assumptions: Start Paired Batches is a new page at /domains/:domainId/start — lean launch form only, no status summary, no domain picker
Domain Status stays at /domains/:domainId/status — monitoring, status drawer, and inline backfill gap detection

Backfill UI (Missing Model Backfill section, BackfillConfirmModal) is deleted — replaced by a link from Status that navigates to the Start page with prefilled params (models, definitions, target depth)
User navigates back to Domains overview to pick a different domain — no domain selector on Start or Status pages
Post-launch redirect: after launching from Start page, user is redirected to Status page with ?evaluationId= highlighted
StatusRedirect (/status) continues to exist but redirects to /domains/:domainId/status instead of /run-trials
The backfillDomainEvaluationModels API mutation stays — the Start page calls it when prefilled with backfill context
discovery.summary: Split the combined DomainTrialsDashboard into two pages: Start Paired Batches (launch) and Domain Status (monitor). Eliminate the separate backfill UI by reusing the Start page with prefilled inputs.

answers.Should the Domains overview 'Add Paired: Yes — update it to link to /domains/:domainId/start. The domain-level launch and backfill are unified on the new Start page. The vignette-level StartPairedBatchPage stays separate (Option B).
answers.Nav structure for the split pages?: No nav entry for Start Batches — users reach it from Domains overview button or Status page fill-gap link. Status stays as top-level nav entry redirecting to /domains/:domainId/status. Nav is unchanged except the redirect target.
answers.How to pass backfill context to Start pa: URL search params: /domains/:domainId/start?evaluationId=abc&models=gpt-4o&depth=5. When evaluationId is present, Start page uses backfillDomainEvaluationModels mutation instead of startDomainEvaluation. Shareable and survives refresh.

non_goals: Unifying vignette-level StartPairedBatchPage with the domain-level Start page
acceptance_criteria: Post-launch redirects to Status with ?evaluationId= highlighted
